resources.string: TouchLock uses the Accessibility Service to intercept Volume Up and Volume Down key presses so you can unlock the screen without touching it. No personal data is accessed or collected.
TouchLock lets you disable all screen touches — perfect for keeping kids from messing with your phone during videos or calls.
TouchLock needs Accessibility access to listen for your secret unlock button combo (Volume Up + Volume Down).
TouchLock draws a transparent shield over your screen to block all touches. This requires the "Display over other apps" permission.
Tap the big lock button on the main screen to activate TouchLock. Press Volume Up + Volume Down together for 2 seconds to unlock.
Not granted yet — tap above to enable
How would you like to unlock the screen?
While locked, should the screen stay on or be allowed to sleep normally?
Touch the button to block all screen input
Press Vol Up + Vol Down for 2s to unlock
Press Vol Up + Vol Down for 2s to unlock
Press Volume Up and Volume Down at the same time and hold for 2 seconds. The screen will unlock immediately.
Why does it need Accessibility permission?
The Accessibility Service is the only Android-approved way to listen for hardware volume key presses when the screen is blocked.
Does it work during video calls?
Yes! The transparent overlay blocks touches but does not block sound, video, or incoming calls.
Will it survive closing the app?
Yes. The lock runs as a Foreground Service, which keeps it alive even when you switch apps or the screen turns off.
Android requires Foreground Services to show a persistent notification. You can minimize or hide it in your notification settings.
Tap to instantly lock the touchscreen
